title: triticum cristatum
side_effects: Triticum cristatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high fiber content. In some cases, excessive consumption of the plant may lead to allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and swelling. Additionally, the high phytate content in Triticum cristatum may interfere with nutrient absorption, potentially causing deficiencies in essential minerals like iron and zinc. If not prepared correctly, the plant's lectins can also cause stomach cramps, bloating, and gas.
